edges.description: B. mallei T6SS-1 activity contributes to multinucleated giant cell formation in infected macrophage monolayers.
edges.source: Intracellular macrophage survival and T6SS-1 actin-based spread

nodes.description: B. mallei is a facultative intracellular pathogen. T6SS-1 supports intracellular growth and actin-based motility after uptake by macrophages, promoting cell-to-cell spread and multinucleated giant cell formation.
nodes.id: Intracellular macrophage survival and T6SS-1 actin-based spread

biological_processes: symbiont-mediated actin polymerization-dependent cell-to-cell migration in host

evidence.reference_title: Burkholderia mallei cluster 1 type VI secretion mutants exhibit growth and actin polymerization defects in RAW 264.7 murine macrophages.
evidence.snippet: facultative intracellular pathogen that causes severe disease in animals and humans.

evidence.reference_title: Burkholderia mallei cluster 1 type VI secretion mutants exhibit growth and actin polymerization defects in RAW 264.7 murine macrophages.
evidence.snippet: significant growth defects were observed in comparison to controls.

evidence.reference_title: Burkholderia mallei cluster 1 type VI secretion mutants exhibit growth and actin polymerization defects in RAW 264.7 murine macrophages.
evidence.snippet: the mutants exhibited actin polymerization defects resulting in inefficient intra- and intercellular spread characteristics.
